question: Critical evaluation of scientific and medical literature
Broad knowledge of intellectual property
Broad knowledge of the clinical genetics and genomics landscape in Ontario/Canada (e.g., services available, access to testing, funding agencies, key payers and payment models, major stakeholders)

Data visualization and figure creation
Tolerance for repetitive / inconclusive, and for troubleshooting inconclusive / failed work
Writing for medical professionals
Writing genetic test reports
Generating and using slides effectively (e.g., PowerPoint, Keynote)
Speaking clearly, effectively, and persuasively
Effectively and succinctly synthesizing / summarizing complex information to generate insights
Complying with rules, guidelines, policies, laws, and regulations
Working effectively in a hierarchical team (e.g., collaboration, sharing timely updates, willingness to take on tasks for the good of the project / team, reporting to a team lead / supervisor)
Managing / leading a team, and / or working with direct reports
Adapting to changing goals, projects, and circumstances at work
Listening / being attentive
Providing constructive feedback

Marketing oneself effectively when seeking and applying for job opportunities (e.g., building a strong professional reputation, pitching oneself to potential employers, standing out from other applicants)
Performing literature searches and reviews
Critically reading scientific and medical literature
Using literature and / or new data to draw conclusions and make hypotheses
Implementing AMP / ASCO and other professional guidelines for somatic / cancer variant analysis
Providing actionable results that can impact patient care
Writing / editing project reports / other business-related correspondence
Researching, analyzing, and / or evaluating existing policy and programs related to genetic services
Writing position papers or policy papers
Maintaining a professional presence on social media (e.g., LinkedIn)
Creating digital content (e.g., newsletters, social media posts, podcasts, webinars, websites)
Creating presentations
Giving presentations
Writing about science for non-scientists
Speaking about science to non-scientists
Speaking about science to scientists
Communicating with / responding to a variety of stakeholders and cross-functional / multidisciplinary team members
Working directly with patients
Building relationships / working directly with clients and / or customers
Advocacy, both personal and on behalf of others, particularly marginalized groups, for acute issues and system-wide change
Providing technical guidance on the application of products and policies related to genetic services
Recruiting and tracking patients in clinical trials, including patient consenting and data collection
Work-related travel
Attending conferences or scientific meetings
Analyzing and managing financial data or budgets
Assessing business trends, strategies, and competitive activity; entrepreneurial ideas
Working on large, scoping, or “big picture” projects
Change management (i.e., dealing with transitions / transformations of goals, processes and / or technologies; moderating change)
